news.headline: WTI Crude +1.03% to $80.16; Brent +1.25% to $85.79 — US-Iran Military Strikes Escalate; Strait of Hormuz Traffic -50%
news.what_happened: The US launched strikes on 90+ Iranian targets over two consecutive days in retaliation for Iranian attacks on oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic fell more than 50% week-over-week. Brent crude hit a one-month high at $85.79. WTI settled at $80.16.
news.why_it_matters: India imports ~80% of its crude oil. Rising Brent above $85 widens India's current account deficit, pressures the rupee, and raises input costs for airlines, paints, tyres, logistics, and chemicals. However, upstream producers (ONGC, OIL) benefit directly from higher per-barrel realization. If conflict escalates to a full Hormuz closure, India macro stress intensifies sharply.

news.headline: US June CPI Cools to 3.5% — Lowest Since 2020; Fed Rate-Hike Bets Ease; Nasdaq +0.90%
news.what_happened: US June 2026 CPI came in at 3.5% year-over-year, matching 2020 lows and beating estimates. Fed rate-hike expectations eased sharply. Growth stocks and semiconductors rallied. Nasdaq closed +0.90%, S&P 500 +0.38%, Dow nearly flat.
news.why_it_matters: Cooling US inflation reduces recession fears and supports corporate IT spending budgets. This is a strong positive signal for Indian IT exporters — enterprise software and services demand outlook improves. USD/INR is flat at 96.19, providing no additional currency tailwind but also no headwind.

news.headline: India CPI June 2026 at 4.38% — First Breach of RBI's 4% Target in 18 Months; Rate Cut Hopes Pushed Back
news.what_happened: India's retail inflation rose to 4.38% in June 2026 (from 3.93% in May), driven by food prices — especially tomatoes and ginger — surging. This is the first breach of the RBI's 4% target since January 2025. RBI kept the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% with a neutral stance, projecting FY27 CPI at 4.6%.
news.why_it_matters: Near-term rate cut expectations are significantly diminished. Rate-sensitive sectors — NBFCs, housing finance, real estate, consumer durables — lose their primary re-rating catalyst. Elevated food inflation also squeezes FMCG margins and could dampen rural consumption if sustained.

news.headline: HCL Technologies -4.64% Post Q1 FY27 — PAT +20.3% But Unchanged Revenue Guidance Disappoints
news.what_happened: HCLTech reported Q1 FY27 PAT of ₹4,624 Cr (+20.3% YoY), revenue ₹34,579 Cr (+13.9%), interim dividend ₹12/share. However, the company maintained its FY27 constant-currency revenue growth guidance at 1%-4%, disappointing analysts who expected a raise. JPMorgan retained 'Underweight'; Jefferies 'Underperform'.
news.why_it_matters: This is a company-specific guidance-conservatism event, NOT a sector-wide IT demand signal. HCLTech's caution reflects its specific mix (telecom, manufacturing) rather than broad IT weakness. TCS, INFY (yet to report Q1) should not be penalised by this news.

news.headline: China Q2 2026 GDP Data Releases Today — Expected at 4.5% vs 5.0% in Q1; Metals at Risk
news.what_happened: China's Q2 2026 GDP is expected to slow to 4.5% YoY from 5.0% in Q1, driven by weak domestic consumption, property sector distress (-4.9% investment YoY), even as industrial output stays solid at +4.6%. Today's data release is a live market-moving event for Asian metals and commodities.
news.why_it_matters: A miss below 4.5% will be negative for Indian metals producers — TATASTEEL, JSWSTEEL, HINDALCO, VEDL — as China is the world's largest steel and aluminium consumer. If a surprise stimulus announcement accompanies the weak print, there could be a sharp short-covering bounce in metals.

news.headline: Asian Markets Rally — Nikkei +0.87%, Hang Seng +0.71%; Risk-On Mood Broadens
news.what_happened: Nikkei 225 closed at 68,332 (+0.87%) and Hang Seng rose to 24,513 (+0.71%) on improved risk sentiment following the US CPI data. Asian markets broadly outperformed despite the crude oil headwind.
news.why_it_matters: Positive Asia cues and falling US 10Y yields (4.585%, -2.4bps) support global risk-on, which typically draws FII flows back into Indian large caps. DII buying of ₹2,928 Cr on July 14 provides a domestic floor.

sector_outlook.reason: Crude +1.03%, Brent $85.79, US-Iran geopolitical risk keeps supply disruption premium elevated. ONGC, OIL benefit directly from higher per-barrel realization.
Nasdaq +0.90%, US CPI 3.5% eases recession fears, improves enterprise tech-spend outlook. HCL Tech drop is company-specific; sector intact.
Crude +1.03% raises jet fuel costs (35-40% of OPEX). INDIGO, SPICEJET face margin compression.
Crude-derived feedstock (40-50% of raw material costs) becoming more expensive. ASIANPAINT, BERGEPAINT headwinds.
Synthetic rubber and carbon black from crude rising. MRF, APOLLOTYRE, CEATLTD input cost pressure.
India CPI 4.38% pushes back rate cut timeline. RBI neutral at 5.25%. Near-term NIM expansion thesis weakens.
Rate cut hopes delayed. DLF, GODREJPROP, LODHA lose key re-rating catalyst near term.
China Q2 GDP expected at 4.5% (slowing). Demand headwinds for TATASTEEL, JSWSTEEL, HINDALCO, VEDL.
Short-term inventory gains from crude rise partially offset by potential auto-fuel price pressure. BPCL, HPCL, IOC.
USD/INR flat eliminates currency tailwind. Defensive characteristics but no fresh trigger. SUNPHARMA outperformed yesterday.
Food inflation rising adds input-cost pressure. Below-normal monsoon adds rural demand risk. HINDUNILVR, ITC relatively defensive.
Crude +1.03% raises fuel operating costs for DELHIVERY, BLUEDART, CONCOR.

picks.catalyst: Brent Crude +1.25% to $85.79 (1-month high) on US-Iran military escalation; Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic down 50%
picks.logic: ONGC's upstream production receives per-barrel realization directly benchmarked to Brent. Every $1/bbl rise adds ~₹700 Cr annually to ONGC's topline. With the US conducting strikes on 90+ Iranian targets and Strait traffic plummeting, the geopolitical supply-disruption premium is not going away today. ONGC carries no input cost exposure to crude (it produces oil, not consumes it) — making it the cleanest beneficiary in the market. Prev close ₹248.90.

picks.catalyst: US June CPI 3.5% (lowest since 2020) → Nasdaq +0.90%, Fed rate-hike bets ease; enterprise IT spending outlook improves
picks.logic: Cooling US inflation is a direct tailwind for IT sector demand — enterprise budgets tighten in recession fear, relax when inflation eases. Nasdaq's 0.90% gain signals strong risk-on tech sentiment globally. TCS as India's largest IT bellwether attracts the first round of FII buying on positive global IT cues. Key distinction: HCL Tech's -4.64% was guidance-specific conservatism unrelated to sector demand — TCS has not yet reported Q1, creating a clean re-rating window. USD/INR flat at 96.19 presents no currency headwind. Prev close ₹2,200.60.

risks: GIFT Nifty flat/slightly negative (-0.09%) — cautious open expected; avoid chasing stocks at premium to watch zones on open
US-Iran conflict could escalate to full Strait of Hormuz closure — Brent spike above $90-95 would be a macro shock for India (CAD widening, INR depreciation, inflation spike)
China Q2 GDP data releases today — a miss below 4.5% will hit metals sector and could drag broader market sentiment
FII were net sellers of ₹740 Cr on July 14 — sustained FII selling could cap upside in large caps including TCS, ONGC
India CPI at 4.38% — if July data also exceeds 4%, RBI may signal a tightening bias at the August MPC, hitting rate-sensitive sectors severely
eod_summary: Both picks missed targets. ONGC -0.69% (target +4%), TCS -0.52% (target +3%). Market sentiment was cautious despite bullish catalysts; FII selling pressure and weak global cues weighed on both picks. Nifty 50 closed marginally higher at 24078.5 (+0.11%).
